title: Reindex detach-before-rename swallows all errors as 'not attached'; no inode backstop in path-string attach check

findingDetails.evidence: detachActiveVectorShard(jobDb) is wrapped in try/catch whose comment states 'Not attached on this connection; the rename is still safe', treating every throw as benign. detachActiveVectorShard (vector-index-store.ts:1664-1674) execs DETACH DATABASE active_vec, which can also throw when the attachment is busy/locked, leaving it LIVE. The only attachment-identity check, get_attached_vector_path (vector-index-store.ts:460-463), reads PRAGMA database_list.file (path string), and the attach fast-path (vector-index-store.ts:1562-1605) compares attachedPath===resolve(shardPath) with no inode comparison.
findingDetails.why: If DETACH ever fails with the attachment still live, fs.renameSync(staging,activeShardPath) at reindex.ts:677 leaves the connection bound to the orphaned old inode while the path string still matches the new file. The re-attach at reindex.ts:737 then hits the path-string fast-path, probes the still-valid OLD shard, passes, and concludes 'already attached'—silently serving stale pre-reindex vectors after the job reports success and flips the active-embedder pointer. There is no inode/file-identity backstop to detect the divergence.
findingDetails.recommendation: After the detach attempt, assert get_attached_vector_path(jobDb)===null before renaming (re-detach or throw otherwise) instead of swallowing all errors as 'not attached'; and/or add a file-identity (fs.statSync ino) check to the attachActiveVectorShard fast-path (vector-index-store.ts:1562) so a path-string match with a changed inode forces a detach+re-attach rather than trusting the stale binding.
